reason: An OS is fundamentally a resource manager for hardware and software; the other options are application-level or peripheral tasks.
'more' is the classic Unix pager showing one screen-full at a time; cat dumps whole file, ls/dir list directories.
'passwd' is the standard Unix command to change a password; other options are not real commands.
'ls' lists directory contents in Unix; 'dir'/'dir /w' are DOS.
'vi' is the POSIX-standard visual editor shipped with every Unix; emacs is common but not standard.
vi has command (edit) mode and insert mode; 'Edit and Insert' is the only option matching that pair.
rlogin is the BSD 'remote login' utility.
A byte is 8 bits by definition on all modern systems.
A cylinder is the set of same-numbered tracks on all platter surfaces, i.e. vertically aligned tracks in a disk pack.
Every device listed in A, B and C (disk, tape, CD, hard disk, optical, floppy) is a data storage device, so 'All the above'.
The OSI reference model defines 7 layers.
DNS's primary job is forward resolution of hostnames to IP addresses; option A describes reverse DNS, a secondary function.
A modem modulates/demodulates: digital to analog and analog to digital conversion.
Format Painter copies formatting from a selected object/text and applies it to what you click next.
Freeze Panes locks the selected rows/columns region so it stays visible while scrolling; A is the only option describing an area-based lock.
Ctrl+C is the standard copy shortcut.
Gridlines are hidden by clearing the gridlines checkbox in Excel options/View; there is no 'hide all' toggle button.
Page Break Preview is an editable view showing where pages break, i.e. how the sheet will print.
